title: GitHub - ThatXliner/NerdLens: Mirror your laptop in VR
excerpt: Mirror your laptop in VR. Contribute to ThatXliner/NerdLens development by creating an account on GitHub.
content_html:
Put your computer screen in a headset you already own.
A wired, open-source VR desktop experiment for iPhone and Google Cardboard.

NerdLens captures your desktop, compresses it into JPEG frames, sends those frames over USB, and renders them on a virtual screen inside a stereoscopic Cardboard view. No expensive headset. No cloud streaming. Just a phone, a cardboard viewer, and an arguably unreasonable amount of curiosity.

Important

NerdLens is an early proof of concept, not a polished consumer app. The current mobile project targets iOS, requires a source build, and is best suited to hacking and experimentation.

What it can do

  • Stream a selected display or window into VR at a configurable target of 10–60 FPS
  • Keep traffic on a wired USB tunnel through iproxy
  • Show a live preview, connection state, frame count, resolution, and target FPS
  • Move the virtual display closer or farther away without leaving the headset
  • Recover from interrupted frame writes with automatic reconnect attempts
  • Keep the iPhone awake during a viewing session

How it works

flowchart LR
    A["Desktop or window"] --> B["Svelte capture UI"]
    B -->|"JPEG frames"| C["Tauri / Rust host"]
    C -->|"TCP :12345"| D["iproxy USB tunnel"]
    D --> E["Unity iOS client"]
    E --> F["Texture on a VR display"]
    F --> G["Google Cardboard"]
    B -->|"distance setting"| C
Loading

The phone runs the TCP server; the desktop host connects to localhost:12345, which iproxy forwards to the attached iPhone. Each message starts with a one-byte type followed by a big-endian payload length and the payload itself.

Type Payload Purpose
0 none Keep-alive, reserved by the client
1 JPEG bytes Replace the texture shown in VR
2 UTF-8 JSON Update viewer settings such as display distance

Quick start

You will need

  • macOS and Xcode
  • An iPhone running iOS 13 or newer
  • A Google Cardboard-compatible viewer
  • A USB cable
  • Unity Hub with Unity 6000.1.5f1 and iOS Build Support
  • Bun, Rust, and the Tauri system prerequisites
  • iproxy from libimobiledevice

Install iproxy with Homebrew:

brew install libimobiledevice

Then clone the project:

git clone https://github.com/ThatXliner/NerdLens.git
cd NerdLens

1. Build the iPhone client

  1. Open the Test0 directory in Unity Hub using Unity 6000.1.5f1.
  2. Open Assets/Scenes/SampleScene.unity.
  3. Switch the build target to iOS and build the Xcode project.
  4. Open the generated project in Xcode, select your development team, connect your iPhone, and run the app on the device.
  5. Place the phone in your Cardboard viewer after the app launches.

The Google Cardboard XR package is already declared in the Unity project and will be fetched by Unity Package Manager.

2. Open the USB tunnel

Connect and trust the iPhone, leave the NerdLens mobile app running, then start the tunnel in a separate terminal:

iproxy 12345 12345

Keep this process running for the entire session.

3. Start the desktop host

cd desktop
bun install --frozen-lockfile
bun run tauri dev

In NerdLens:

  1. Select Connect.
  2. Select Start Recording and choose a display or window.
  3. Put on the headset.
  4. Use the Distance slider to place the screen comfortably.

That is it. Your monitor is now wearing a monitor.

Development

Run these commands from desktop/:

# Start the Svelte frontend only
bun run dev

# Type-check the frontend
bun run check

# Build the frontend
bun run build

# Run the complete desktop app
bun run tauri dev

# Validate the Rust host
cargo check --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml

Project map

NerdLens/
├── desktop/                       # Tauri 2 + Svelte 5 screen-streaming host
│   ├── src/routes/+page.svelte   # Capture controls, preview, and statistics
│   └── src-tauri/src/lib.rs      # TCP protocol, retries, and Tauri commands
├── Test0/                         # Unity 6 iOS/Cardboard client
│   ├── Assets/ReceiveVideo.cs    # TCP server and frame/settings decoder
│   └── Assets/Scenes/            # VR scene
├── JOURNAL.md                     # Original build log
└── Logo.png

Troubleshooting

The host says it cannot connect

  • Launch the iPhone app before selecting Connect.
  • Confirm the phone is unlocked, trusted, and visible over USB.
  • Make sure iproxy 12345 12345 is still running.
  • Check that nothing else is using local port 12345.

The iOS build will not sign

  • Open the generated Xcode project and choose your Apple development team.
  • Give the app a unique bundle identifier if Xcode reports a conflict.
  • Confirm that the connected phone is enabled for development.

The stream freezes or stops

  • Stop and restart screen capture in the desktop host.
  • Restart iproxy, then disconnect and reconnect from NerdLens.
  • Try a lower target FPS; full-resolution JPEG encoding is intentionally simple in this prototype.

The view is uncomfortable

  • Adjust Distance before putting the phone back into the viewer.
  • Take regular breaks. This prototype does not yet include a complete comfort-calibration flow.

Roadmap

  • Smoother, lower-latency video transport
  • Better connection and error feedback on both ends
  • Automatic device discovery and iproxy management
  • Display placement controls and axis locking
  • A more polished VR environment
  • Packaged desktop and mobile releases

The wonderfully candid development story—including the failed first Unity project—is in the project journal.

Contributing

Bug reports, experiments, protocol improvements, and delightfully overengineered ideas are welcome. Open an issue or send a pull request with a clear explanation of what changed and how you tested it.

License

NerdLens is free software released under the GNU General Public License v3.0.

No cloud streaming. Just a phone, a cardboard viewer, and an arguably unreasonable amount of curiosity. ImportantNerdLens is an early proof of concept, not a polished consumer app. The current mobile project targets iOS, requires a source build, and is best suited to hacking and experimentation. blocks.html_en:
Put your computer screen in a headset you already own.
A wired, open-source VR desktop experiment for iPhone and Google Cardboard.

sentences.en: Put your computer screen in a headset you already own.
sentences.zh: 将计算机屏幕放入您已有的耳机中。

sentences.en: A wired, open-source VR desktop experiment for iPhone and Google Cardboard.
sentences.zh: 适用于 iPhone 和 Google Cardboard 的有线开源 VR 桌面实验。

blocks.html_en: NerdLens captures your desktop, compresses it into JPEG frames, sends those frames over USB, and renders them on a virtual screen inside a stereoscopic Cardboard view. No expensive headset. No cloud streaming. Just a phone, a cardboard viewer, and an arguably unreasonable amount of curiosity.

sentences.en: NerdLens captures your desktop, compresses it into JPEG frames, sends those frames over USB, and renders them on a virtual screen inside a stereoscopic Cardboard view.
sentences.zh: NerdLens 捕获您的桌面，将其压缩为 JPEG 帧，通过 USB 发送这些帧，并将它们渲染在立体 Cardboard 视图内的虚拟屏幕上。

sentences.en: No expensive headset.
sentences.zh: 没有昂贵的耳机。

sentences.en: No cloud streaming.
sentences.zh: 没有云流。

sentences.en: Just a phone, a cardboard viewer, and an arguably unreasonable amount of curiosity.
sentences.zh: 只是一部手机、一个纸板观察器和一种可以说是不合理的好奇心。

blocks.html_en: Important

sentences.en: Important
sentences.zh: 重要的

blocks.html_en: NerdLens is an early proof of concept, not a polished consumer app. The current mobile project targets iOS, requires a source build, and is best suited to hacking and experimentation.

sentences.en: NerdLens is an early proof of concept, not a polished consumer app.
sentences.zh: NerdLens 是一个早期的概念验证，而不是一个完善的消费者应用程序。

sentences.en: The current mobile project targets iOS, requires a source build, and is best suited to hacking and experimentation.
sentences.zh: 当前的移动项目针对 iOS，需要源代码构建，并且最适合黑客攻击和实验。

sentences.en: What it can do
sentences.zh: 它能做什么

sentences.en: Stream a selected display or window into VR at a configurable target of 10–60 FPS
sentences.zh: 以 10-60 FPS 的可配置目标将选定的显示器或窗口流式传输到 VR 中

blocks.html_en: • Keep traffic on a wired USB tunnel through iproxy

sentences.en: Keep traffic on a wired USB tunnel through iproxy
sentences.zh: 通过 iproxy 将流量保持在有线 USB 隧道上

blocks.html_en: • Show a live preview, connection state, frame count, resolution, and target FPS

sentences.en: Show a live preview, connection state, frame count, resolution, and target FPS
sentences.zh: 显示实时预览、连接状态、帧数、分辨率和目标 FPS

blocks.html_en: • Move the virtual display closer or farther away without leaving the headset

sentences.en: Move the virtual display closer or farther away without leaving the headset
sentences.zh: 在不离开耳机的情况下将虚拟显示器移近或移远

blocks.html_en: • Recover from interrupted frame writes with automatic reconnect attempts

sentences.en: Recover from interrupted frame writes with automatic reconnect attempts
sentences.zh: 通过自动重新连接尝试从中断的帧写入中恢复

blocks.html_en: • Keep the iPhone awake during a viewing session

sentences.en: Keep the iPhone awake during a viewing session
sentences.zh: 在观看会话期间保持 iPhone 处于唤醒状态

sentences.en: How it works

sentences.zh: 电话运行 TCP 服务器；桌面主机连接到 localhost:12345，iproxy 将其转发到连接的 iPhone。

sentences.en: Each message starts with a one-byte type followed by a big-endian payload length and the payload itself.
sentences.zh: 每条消息都以一字节类型开头，后跟大端有效负载长度和有效负载本身。

blocks.html_en: Bug reports, experiments, protocol improvements, and delightfully overengineered ideas are welcome. Open an issue or send a pull request with a clear explanation of what changed and how you tested it.

content_html_zh:
将计算机屏幕放入您已有的耳机中。 适用于 iPhone 和 Google Cardboard 的有线开源 VR 桌面实验。

NerdLens 捕获您的桌面，将其压缩为 JPEG 帧，通过 USB 发送这些帧，并将它们渲染在立体 Cardboard 视图内的虚拟屏幕上。 没有昂贵的耳机。 没有云流。 只是一部手机、一个纸板观察器和一种可以说是不合理的好奇心。

重要的

NerdLens 是一个早期的概念验证，而不是一个完善的消费者应用程序。 当前的移动项目针对 iOS，需要源代码构建，并且最适合黑客攻击和实验。

它能做什么

  • 以 10-60 FPS 的可配置目标将选定的显示器或窗口流式传输到 VR 中
  • 通过 iproxy 将流量保持在有线 USB 隧道上
  • 显示实时预览、连接状态、帧数、分辨率和目标 FPS
  • 在不离开耳机的情况下将虚拟显示器移近或移远
  • 通过自动重新连接尝试从中断的帧写入中恢复
  • 在观看会话期间保持 iPhone 处于唤醒状态

它是如何运作的

flowchart LR
    A["Desktop or window"] --> B["Svelte capture UI"]
    B -->|"JPEG frames"| C["Tauri / Rust host"]
    C -->|"TCP :12345"| D["iproxy USB tunnel"]
    D --> E["Unity iOS client"]
    E --> F["Texture on a VR display"]
    F --> G["Google Cardboard"]
    B -->|"distance setting"| C
Loading

电话运行 TCP 服务器；桌面主机连接到 localhost:12345，iproxy 将其转发到连接的 iPhone。 每条消息都以一字节类型开头，后跟大端有效负载长度和有效负载本身。

Type 有效载荷 目的
0 none Keep-alive，由客户端保留
1 JPEG 字节 替换VR中显示的纹理
2 UTF-8 JSON 更新查看器设置，例如显示距离

快速启动

你将需要

  • macOS 和 Xcode
  • 运行 iOS 13 或更高版本的 iPhone
  • 兼容 Google Cardboard 的查看器
  • USB 电缆
  • Unity Hub 具有 Unity 6000.1.5f1 和 iOS 构建支持
  • Bun、Rust 和 Tauri 系统先决条件
  • 来自 libimobiledevice 的 iproxy

使用 Homebrew 安装 iproxy：

brew install libimobiledevice

然后克隆项目：

git clone https://github.com/ThatXliner/NerdLens.git
cd NerdLens

1. 构建 iPhone 客户端

  1. 使用 Unity 6000.1.5f1 打开 Unity Hub 中的 Test0 目录。
  2. 打开 Assets/Scenes/SampleScene.unity。
  3. 将构建目标切换到 iOS 并构建 Xcode 项目。
  4. 在 Xcode 中打开生成的项目，选择您的开发团队，连接您的 iPhone，然后在设备上运行应用程序。
  5. 应用程序启动后，将手机放入 Cardboard 查看器中。

Google Cardboard XR 包已在 Unity 项目中声明，并将由 Unity 包管理器获取。

2. 打开USB隧道

连接并信任 iPhone，让 NerdLens 移动应用保持运行，然后在单独的终端中启动隧道：

iproxy 12345 12345

在整个会话期间保持此进程运行。

3. 启动桌面主机

cd desktop
bun install --frozen-lockfile
bun run tauri dev

在 NerdLens 中：

  1. 选择连接。
  2. 选择开始录制并选择显示或窗口。
  3. 戴上耳机。
  4. 使用距离滑块舒适地放置屏幕。

就是这样。 您的显示器现在已佩戴显示器。

发展

从桌面/运行这些命令：

# Start the Svelte frontend only
bun run dev

# Type-check the frontend
bun run check

# Build the frontend
bun run build

# Run the complete desktop app
bun run tauri dev

# Validate the Rust host
cargo check --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml

项目地图

NerdLens/
├── desktop/                       # Tauri 2 + Svelte 5 screen-streaming host
│   ├── src/routes/+page.svelte   # Capture controls, preview, and statistics
│   └── src-tauri/src/lib.rs      # TCP protocol, retries, and Tauri commands
├── Test0/                         # Unity 6 iOS/Cardboard client
│   ├── Assets/ReceiveVideo.cs    # TCP server and frame/settings decoder
│   └── Assets/Scenes/            # VR scene
├── JOURNAL.md                     # Original build log
└── Logo.png

故障排除

主机说无法连接

  • 在选择“连接”之前启动 iPhone 应用程序。
  • 确认手机已解锁、受信任并且通过 USB 可见。
  • 确保 iproxy 12345 12345 仍在运行。
  • 检查没有其他东西正在使用本地端口 12345。

iOS 版本不会签名

  • 打开生成的 Xcode 项目并选择您的 Apple 开发团队。
  • 如果 Xcode 报告冲突，请为应用程序提供唯一的包标识符。
  • 确认连接的手机已启用开发。

流冻结或停止

  • 停止并重新启动桌面主机中的屏幕捕获。
  • 重新启动 iproxy，然后断开与 NerdLens 的连接并重新连接。
  • 尝试较低的目标 FPS；在此原型中，全分辨率 JPEG 编码故意变得简单。

景色不舒服

  • 在将手机放回查看器之前调整距离。
  • 定期休息。 该原型尚不包括完整的舒适度校准流程。

路线图

  • 更流畅、更低延迟的视频传输
  • 两端更好的连接和错误反馈
  • 自动设备发现和 IP 代理管理
  • 显示放置控制和轴锁定
  • 更精致的 VR 环境
  • 打包的桌面和移动版本

项目日志中包含了极其坦率的开发故事（包括失败的第一个 Unity 项目）。

贡献

欢迎错误报告、实验、协议改进和令人愉快的过度设计想法。 打开问题或发送拉取请求，并明确说明更改内容以及测试方式。

执照

NerdLens 是根据 GNU 通用公共许可证 v3.0 发布的免费软件。
